- [docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/functions/vm_search.mdx](docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/functions/vm_search.mdx): Explains the administrator workflow for quickly locating virtual machines by cluster after virtualization is enabled. It describes filtering or searching by VM name, IP address, or creator under Virtualization Management, then opening the VM details page to perform operations such as scaling or troubleshooting.
- [docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/functions/vnc.mdx](docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/functions/vnc.mdx): Explains how to use ACP Web Console VNC login as an emergency access method for a virtual machine. It covers opening VNC from the Virtual Machines list, logging in with the VM username and password, and using console features such as common keyboard commands plus copy and paste.
- [docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/how_to/create-template.mdx](docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/how_to/create-template.mdx): Shows how to convert an existing configured VM into a reusable template for rapid deployment. The workflow installs cloud-init and qemu-guest-agent, cleans cloud-init data, creates a VM snapshot, finds the VolumeSnapshot resource, creates a CDI DataSource in kube-public with ACP image metadata labels such as OS architecture, storage class, access mode, size, and volume mode, then creates new VMs from that image instance.
- [docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/how_to/vm_availability.mdx](docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/how_to/vm_availability.mdx): Explains high availability for ACP virtual machines using Self Node Remediation and Node Health Check to automate node failure detection, fencing, remediation, reboot, resource deletion, and workload rescheduling. It covers installing the Alauda Build of SelfNodeRemediation and NodeHealthCheck Operators, configuring SelfNodeRemediationConfig and SelfNodeRemediationTemplate strategies, creating NodeHealthCheck resources with minHealthy and selectors, and validating VM recovery after node failure.
- [docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/how_to/vm_clone.mdx](docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/how_to/vm_clone.mdx): Explains how to clone KubeVirt virtual machines with the VirtualMachineClone API, requiring snapshot-capable storage with volume snapshots enabled. It provides a quick clone manifest and kubectl workflow, covers status phases such as SnapshotInProgress, CreatingTargetVM, RestoreInProgress, Succeeded, and Failed, and notes how to correct the ACP UI DataVolume label vm.cpaas.io/used-by on cloned disks.
- [docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/how_to/vm_gpust.mdx](docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/how_to/vm_gpust.mdx): Provides environment preparation for physical NVIDIA GPU passthrough to ACP virtual machines, including ARM64 limitations, required gpu-operator charts and images, IOMMU setup, NVIDIA driver removal, gpu-system namespace creation, gpu-operator deployment, and pass-through GPU discovery. It explains how to patch KubeVirt HyperConverged with DisableMDEVConfiguration and permittedHostDevices using pciDeviceSelector and resourceName values, then verifies the GPU model appears during VM creation and includes cleanup steps for deleting VMs, removing KubeVirt GPU configuration, and uninstalling gpu-operator.
- [docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/how_to/vm_precopy.mdx](docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/how_to/vm_precopy.mdx): Explains ACP virtual machine hot migration using KubeVirt's default pre-copy memory migration approach, where memory pages are copied iteratively before a brief final pause and resume on the target host. It covers deployment prerequisites for kubevirt-operator and HyperConverged, recommends matching hardware, Kube-OVN underlay networking, Block mode CephRBD storage, bridge networking, the kubevirt.io/allow-pod-bridge-network-live-migration annotation, and starting migration from the UI while monitoring status and events.
- [docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/how_to/vm_repair.mdx](docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/how_to/vm_repair.mdx): Describes rescue-mode recovery for ACP virtual machines that cannot boot because of problems such as bad fstab changes or filesystem errors requiring fsck. It explains how to obtain an image repository address, stop the VM, edit the VM YAML to boot from a containerDisk with lower bootOrder while preserving the original rootfs as another disk, mount or repair the original filesystem from inside the rescue environment, then restore the original YAML.
- [docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/how_to/vm_usb_passthrough.mdx](docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/how_to/vm_usb_passthrough.mdx): Explains USB host passthrough for ACP virtual machines so applications can access dedicated USB devices such as encryption dongles, peripherals, or smart cards. It requires ACP v3.18 or later and shows how to expose USB devices in the HyperConverged CR under spec.permittedHostDevices.usbHostDevices using vendor and product IDs from lsusb, reference the ResourceName from VM hostDevices, and verify passthrough with lsusb inside the VM.
- [docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/intro.mdx](docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/intro.mdx): Introduces ACP Virtualization virtual machine management built on KubeVirt CRDs such as VirtualMachine and VirtualMachineInstance. It explains that ACP adds a Web Console on top of KubeVirt to make VM operations easier for users.
- [docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/trouble_shooting/vm_migration.mdx](docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/trouble_shooting/vm_migration.mdx): Explains why ACP virtual machine Pods do not automatically migrate after graceful node shutdown or abnormal node crashes, because KubeVirt cannot safely distinguish VM failure from connectivity loss without risking duplicate VM instances. It provides manual recovery commands for draining virt-launcher Pods before planned shutdown, uncordoning nodes afterward, forcibly deleting virt-launcher Pods and VolumeAttachments after crashes, and removing virt-api or virt-controller Pods if they were on the failed node.
- [docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/trouble_shooting/vm_precopy.mdx](docs/en/virtualization/virtualization/virtual_machine/trouble_shooting/vm_precopy.mdx): Lists common hot migration error messages, their causes, and corrective actions for ACP virtual machines. It covers unsupported network configurations requiring Kube-OVN or the kubevirt.io/allow-pod-bridge-network-live-migration annotation, storage failures caused by PVCs that are not ReadWriteMany or shared, non-shared HostDisk limitations, and guidance to recreate VMs with suitable RWX storage such as CephRBD block storage when immutable creation parameters are wrong.
